Article Title: 'We are not ice and fire, we are fire and fire': Sanju Samson on partnership with Abhishek Sharma
Article Text:
Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma (ICC Photo)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

Suryakumar Yadav receives grand homecoming after India’s T20 World Cup win

Poll Who do you think was the standout player in the T20 World Cup final? Sanju Samson Abhishek Sharma

NEW DELHI: Star openers Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma were at their majestic best in the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. The duo tore into the New Zealand bowlers in the title clash, putting on a 98-run opening partnership to give India a flying start in the powerplay.They laid a solid foundation and helped India post a mammoth 255/5 in the final.India then bowled New Zealand out for just 159, sealing a commanding 96-run victory to lift the trophy. It was India’s third T20 World Cup title.India created history by securing a record-breaking third T20 World Cup crown and becoming the first team to successfully defend the title and win it as the host nation.Samson praised his opening partner Abhishek, describing their partnership as effortless and balanced.He highlighted their combination as “fire and fire”, with both players taking turns to lead the attack.Samson also pointed out the natural Kerala-Punjabi friendship that strengthens their understanding."We are not ice and fire, we are fire and fire. Sometimes he fires, sometimes I do. We have that kind of combination going. We have done this since 2024; we have a Kerala-Punjabi friendship in the middle. Everything comes pretty naturally to us, so we do not complicate it.He asks me, 'How is the ball coming?' I tell him that the ball is coming normally, and hit it for a six. It is really simple with him. Abhishek is very brave and collected. I love his character. I really love the partnership with him on and off the field," Sanju Samson said at the India Today Conclave.Samson scripted a remarkable comeback during the T20 World Cup as he battled inconsistency and time on the bench to emerge as India’s leading run-scorer in the tournament. He delivered clutch performances when it mattered most, including a string of half-centuries from the virtual quarterfinal against West Indies to the title clash against New Zealand.Samson scored 321 runs in five innings at an average of 80.25 and a strike rate of 199.37. He smashed 27 fours and 24 sixes and finished as the third-highest run-scorer in the T20 World Cup.
Article Summary: Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma were at their majestic best in the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand. The duo tore into the New Zealand bowlers in the title clash, putting on a 98-run opening partnership to give India a flying start. India then bowled New Zealand out for just 159, sealing a commanding 96-run victory to lift the trophy.

Article Title: 'He didn't have a great time as a player, but he lifted the World Cup': Ricky Ponting on Suryakumar Yadav
Article Text:
Image credit: BCCI

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

Suryakumar Yadav receives grand homecoming after India’s T20 World Cup win

NEW DELHI: Big tournaments often reveal the finer aspects of leadership, and former Australia captain Ricky Ponting observed plenty of that in the way India navigated their ICC Men’s T20 World Cup 2026 campaign under skipper Suryakumar Yadav India entered the tournament as favourites, carrying high expectations while also dealing with form concerns within the squad. The captain himself endured a difficult campaign with the bat. Apart from a rescue knock of 84* off 49 balls in the opener against USA, Yadav scored 158 runs across the remaining eight innings. Despite that, he guided the team to peak at the most crucial moments.Ponting believes the true measure of captaincy often lies behind closed doors."It's about a lot more than what they do off the field, the things that people don't see and how they interact with their players," Ponting said as per the ICC website.The Australian great also pointed out that leading a side becomes even tougher when a captain’s personal form dips, something Yadav experienced during the tournament."He didn't have a great time himself as a player, but still he's standing at the end holding up a World Cup trophy," Ponting noted."I know, as a former captain, when you're not batting at your absolute best, captaincy can become really difficult. And when you are batting well, captaincy can become really easy."Ponting also highlighted how Yadav managed players under pressure, especially those struggling with form and confidence."It would have been really interesting to see how Surya has interacted with Abhishek (Sharma) and Sanju (Samson) over the last few weeks. That's where the real stories will come out on true leadership," he added. Abhishek Sharma , the ICC No. 1-ranked T20I batter, had a turbulent run during the tournament. He registered three consecutive ducks, followed by low scores of 15, 10 and 9, and managed only one fifty before the final. However, on the biggest stage of the tournament, he found his form again, smashing 52 off 21 balls and recording the fastest fifty of the 2026 edition in just 18 deliveries."You don't have to worry about the staples, it's more the guys on the fringe and the younger guys going up and down with their emotions and battling with their form, the ones you have to spend the most time with," he noted.A key tournament decision came at the top of the order when Sanju Samson was given renewed backing. His first opportunity came early in the campaign as cover for Sharma, but he was dropped again before being recalled for a crucial fixture against Zimbabwe. Samson seized the opportunity and turned it into a dominant run.A blistering 97* in a virtual knockout against West Indies sparked his surge. He followed it up with scores of 89 (42) in the semifinal against England and 89 (46) in the final against New Zealand. In just five innings, Samson amassed 321 runs — the most by an Indian in a single T20 World Cup.Ponting emphasised the importance of India’s faith in the wicketkeeper-batter."For India to stick with him at the top there, that was a big call to make. A big decision, but one that worked out really well in the end," he said. "If you get the backing of the coaching staff and the captain, then that's all you need... Just a pat on the back or an arm around the shoulder to say, 'we're sticking with you, we believe in you.""When you've got the quality that Sanju's got, and the confidence of the captain and coach behind you, that's when great things can happen," he concluded.
Article Summary: Suryakumar Yadav captained India to T20 World Cup 2026 victory. Ricky Ponting believes the true measure of captaincy lies behind closed doors. Ponting also highlighted how Yadav managed players under pressure, especially those struggling with form and confidence.

Article Title: Indian players were 'jealous' of Abhishek Sharma: Sanju Samson opens up
Article Text:
Abhishek Sharma (ICC Photo)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

Suryakumar Yadav receives grand homecoming after India’s T20 World Cup win

Poll Should Sanju Samson play a larger leadership role in the Indian cricket team? Yes No

NEW DELHI: India’s star opener Sanju Samson praised his opening partner Abhishek Sharma , describing their partnership as effortless and well-balanced. He said their combination is like “fire and fire”, with both players taking turns to lead the attack. Samson also spoke about their natural Kerala-Punjabi bond that strengthens their understanding. He admired Abhishek’s bravery and composure and said he enjoys their camaraderie both on and off the field."We are not ice and fire, we are fire and fire. Sometimes he fires, sometimes I do. We have that kind of combination going. We have done this since 2024; we have a Kerala-Punjabi friendship in the middle. Everything comes pretty naturally to us, so we do not complicate it.He asks me, 'How is the ball coming?' I tell him that the ball is coming normally, and hit it for a six. It is really simple with him. Abhishek is very brave and collected. I love his character. I really love the partnership with him on and off the field," Sanju Samson said at the India Today Conclave.Samson also revealed that Indian players were once ‘jealous’ of Abhishek because of his early stardom."Earlier, the boys used to get jealous because of the cheers for Abhishek.It was Abhishek, Abhishek everywhere. We were like, do people only see him or what? But yes, he is the superstar of the team now. Get married soon, bhai," he added.Samson and Abhishek tore into the New Zealand bowlers in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final, putting on a 98-run opening partnership to give India a flying start in the powerplay. Their explosive beginning helped India post a massive total of 255 in the final.India created history by securing a record third T20 World Cup title and becoming the first team to successfully defend the trophy and win it on home soil, following a dominant 96-run victory over New Zealand at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad on Sunday.Samson scripted a remarkable comeback during the T20 World Cup, overcoming inconsistency and time on the bench to finish as India’s leading run-scorer in the tournament. He delivered crucial performances, including a string of half-centuries from the virtual quarterfinal against West Indies to the title clash against New Zealand.Samson scored 321 runs in five innings at an average of 80.25 and a strike rate of 199.37. He smashed 27 fours and 24 sixes and finished as the third-highest run-scorer of the T20 World Cup.
Article Summary: India's star opener Sanju Samson praised his opening partner Abhishek Sharma. Samson said their combination is like ‘fire and fire’, with both players taking turns to lead the attack. Samson also spoke about their natural Kerala-Punjabi bond that strengthens their understanding.

Article Title: Iraq football team to travel for FIFA World Cup playoff in Mexico
Article Text:
Iraq head coach Graham Arnold (Image credit: X)

EXCLUSIVE: Rahul Dravid on iconic Eden Gardens win against Australia in 2001

Iraq will travel to Mexico for a 2026 World Cup playoff match despite calls for it to be postponed due to the ongoing Middle East war, the country’s football association said on Saturday."The national team will depart at the end of the week to Mexico via a private plane," Iraq Football Association president Adnan Dirjal said in a statement, adding that they had contacted FIFA to help facilitate the trip amid the regional conflict that has disrupted flights."We succeeded in communicating with some of the clubs of our professional players to facilitate their joining the national team."The match is scheduled to take place on March 31 in the Mexican city of Monterrey.Iraq is set to face either Suriname or Bolivia in the intercontinental playoff final.The winner will be placed in Group I alongside France, Senegal and Norway in the first round of this summer’s men’s tournament, which will be jointly hosted by the United States, Canada and Mexico.The war, triggered by US-Israeli strikes on February 28, has also cast uncertainty over Iran’s participation in the global event.US President Donald Trump warned on Thursday that the Iranian team’s "life and safety" could be at risk in the United States, despite FIFA president Gianni Infantino stating they would be welcome at the tournament.The conflict has also raised concerns over Iraq’s participation, given the country’s proximity to Iran and potential travel disruptions, with the team’s manager earlier urging FIFA to postpone the playoff match.Iraq head coach, Australian Graham Arnold, said several players and members of the technical staff were stranded in the Middle East because of the ongoing conflict.
Article Summary: Iraq will travel to Mexico for a 2026 World Cup playoff match. The match is scheduled to take place on March 31 in the Mexican city of Monterrey. Iraq is set to face either Suriname or Bolivia in the intercontinental playoff final.

Article Title: 'India's DNA is to win trophies, ours is to lose': Rashid Latif tears into Pakistan cricket - WATCH
Article Text:
AFP Photo

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

Suryakumar Yadav receives grand homecoming after India’s T20 World Cup win

Poll Should the Pakistan Cricket Board be held accountable for the team's T20 World Cup exit? Yes, they should be accountable No, it’s the players’ responsibility

NEW DELHI: Pakistan’s poor performance in the recently concluded T20 World Cup continues to attract criticism from various quarters. Former Pakistan captain Rashid Latif has now launched a sharp attack on the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB).Pakistan crashed out of the T20 World Cup after reaching the Super 8 stage of the tournament.Meanwhile, their arch-rivals India successfully defended the T20 World Cup title by defeating New Zealand in the final at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.Latif, who played 37 Tests and 166 ODIs for Pakistan, said that while Pakistan’s DNA is to lose, India have developed a habit of winning major ICC tournaments."Even after losing the toss, they made 250 runs. Nobody will be able to catch them.They have not done this overnight; it is the result of their hard work over the last 10 years or so. See the last ICC events or the Asia Cup," Latif told reporters."India's DNA is to win trophies, and our DNA is to lose even before the knockouts. Their DNA is playing the final and winning," he added.Latif also took a dig at the PCB over the alleged mismanagement of player contracts, calling it a "weak board.""Weaker boards do this. When you sign a contract, it has everything.Fines and rewards are also mentioned," said Latif.India won the T20 World Cup title after defeating New Zealand by 96 runs in the final at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday.The victory made India the first team to win three T20 World Cup titles and also the first to successfully defend the trophy on home soil.
Article Summary: Pakistan crashed out of the T20 World Cup after reaching the Super 8 stage of the tournament. Former Pakistan captain Rashid Latif has now launched a sharp attack on the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) Latif also took a dig at the PCB over the alleged mismanagement of player contracts.

Article Title: '8–0': Pakistan selector Aaqib Javed revives Shadab Khan's dig at legends during T20 World Cup
Article Text:
India vs Pakistan (AP Photo)

IPL 2026 should be audition for the next India T20I captain

NEW DELHI: Officials from the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) on Saturday held a press conference to address the team’s performance in the T20 World Cup, where India national cricket team went on to win the title.During the press conference, Pakistan selector Aaqib Javed spoke about the team’s results and also referred to the long-standing record against India in ODI World Cups.Speaking alongside former Pakistan players Misbah-ul-Haq , Sarfaraz Ahmed and Asad Shafiq, Javed said Pakistan should not keep using the India record as an excuse."Not being able to beat India in the World Cup is not an excuse, the result there is 8–0, and even the world’s best captains have played from 1975 to 2026, so set that aside.In the Super 8 stage, one match was washed out due to rain, and we lost one match nothing else happened," he said."Against Sri Lanka, winning by that margin wasn’t achievable. We lost only one match. We were eliminated only on net run rate. Making it such a huge issue is too much. It’s a big issue, but not so big that our cricket is destroyed or that we can’t compete anywhere in the world."The discussion also brought back earlier controversy involving Pakistan all-rounder Shadab Khan .During the tournament, Shadab had strongly responded to criticism from former players after Pakistan’s defeat to India in Colombo.When asked about remarks from ex-cricketers such as Shahid Afridi and Mohammad Yousuf, he pointed out that their teams had never beaten India in World Cups, unlike Pakistan’s 2021 T20 World Cup victory over India. Shadab was part of the 2021 side."Former cricketers have their own opinions. They (former players) were legends, but even they could not achieve what we have. We have beaten India in a World Cup," he added.The message also carried nothing but a lack of respect for past players, including Shadab’s father-in-law Saqlain Mushtaq .Saqlain himself expressed disappointment with the remarks."Those were unwanted comments, and most of the players had played with me. In fact, I am also one of them who had not won an ICC event, but we had won several big matches for Pakistan, Tests and ODIs," Saqlain said on a Pakistani show.
Article Summary: Pakistan Cricket Board held a press conference to address the team’s performance in the T20 World Cup. Pakistan selector Aaqib Javed referred to the long- standing record against India in ODI World Cups. Javed said Pakistan should not keep using the India record as an excuse.

Article Title: xQc net worth in 2026: How Félix Lengyel built a $50 million fortune through streaming, Kick deal and esports career
Article Text:
xQc net worth in 2026: How Félix Lengyel built a $50 million fortune through streaming, Kick deal and esports career (Image via Getty)

How xQc built his net worth through Twitch streaming, esports career, sponsorships and the Kick deal





Félix Lengyel, better known online as xQc, is one of the most popular streamers in the world today. Millions of people watch him play games, react to videos, and chat with fans live online. Because of that huge audience, many fans often wonder how much money he has made over the years. Several reports say the Canadian streamer has built massive wealth through streaming, esports, sponsorship deals, and platform contracts. A report from Celebrity Net Worth says Félix Lengyel’s estimated net worth is about $50 million.His rise did not happen overnight. xQc first became known as a professional Overwatch player before moving fully into streaming.Over time, his audience grew, turning him into one of the biggest streamers on Twitch and later on Kick. He achieved one of his most important career milestones when he signed a two-year non-exclusive streaming contract with Kick in June 2023. The contract had a base value of $70 million but included performance-based bonuses which could increase the total to $100 million according to reports.xQc started his esports career through professional Overwatch competition.He participated in significant tournaments during that period and developed a reputation for his dynamic character.After stepping away from competitive play, he focused completely on streaming. His decision turned out to be the biggest step in building his wealth.Over the years, xQc streamed thousands of hours on Twitch. Reports say he has streamed more than 23,000 hours, which helped him become one of the platform’s most subscribed creators.A major leak in 2020, discussed by YouTuber Veil, revealed how much some top Twitch streamers were earning. Veil explained that the leak showed Félix Lengyel had made more than $8 million from Twitch between 2019 and 2021.Veil said:“In 2020, a Twitch earnings leak revealed that he made $8.4 million over two years, just from Twitch.”Streaming is not his only income source. xQc has also worked with sponsors and brands. One of the known partnerships includes the energy drink company G-Fuel, which often collaborates with gaming creators.Another big part of the discussion around his finances involves online gambling streams. Reports from late 2024 said Félix Lengyel had wagered nearly $3 billion on the online casino platform Stake.com through his partnership with the company.Fellow Twitch streamer Matthew “Mizkif” Rinaudo once spoke about how surprising Félix Lengyel’s wealth appears from the outside.Mizkif said:“It is mind-boggling to understand how much money this guy really has at this point. I have seen everybody from low to high, and seeing xQc and how that man gambles is the most mind explosion. It almost does not seem real.”He also added:“It seems like you are playing GTA watching this guy just mess around. He will pull out 100K like you would pull out a dollar.”Today, with streaming contracts, sponsorship deals, and years of online success behind him, xQc remains one of the richest and most watched gaming creators on the internet.
Article Summary: Félix Lengyel, better known online as xQc, is one of the most popular streamers in the world today. Millions of people watch him play games, react to videos, and chat with fans live online. Several reports say the Canadian streamer has built massive wealth through streaming, esports, sponsorship deals, and platform contracts.

Article Title: Paul Skenes: "Spoon fed": Paul Skenes breaks silence after Bubba Chandler questions Pirates ace’s rapid rise to MLB spotlight
Article Text:
"Spoon fed" Paul Skenes breaks silence after Bubba Chandler questions Pirates ace’s rapid rise to MLB spotlight (Image via Getty Images)

Pittsburgh Pirates pitcher Paul Skenes responds after teammate Bubba Chandler jokes he was “spoon fed” in fast MLB rise

Paul Skenes and Bubba Chandler created a funny moment that quickly caught fans’ attention. The Pittsburgh Pirates teammates were joking about Skenes’ fast rise in Major League Baseball, and Chandler did not hold back during the interview. Chandler teased the Pirates ace by saying Skenes was “spoon fed” because he reached the major leagues so quickly. His comment surprised many fans, but it was clearly meant as friendly locker room banter. When Skenes later heard about the remark, he gave a calm but funny reply.The moment showed the light side of the Pirates' clubhouse. Even though Skenes is now one of the most talked about pitchers in baseball, his teammates still enjoy pulling his leg.The young pitcher has become a huge name since making his MLB debut with the Pittsburgh Pirates in 2024. His performances quickly turned him into one of the league’s most exciting arms. But as Chandler’s joke showed, inside the clubhouse Skenes is still just another teammate who can be teased like everyone else.During the interview, Bubba Chandler explained how Pirates players often joke with Paul Skenes about how quickly he reached the major leagues.Chandler said the team enjoys reminding Skenes about being the first overall pick in the MLB draft and how little time he spent in the minor leagues.“We always bust his chops on, you know, he’s spoon-fed,” Chandler said. “One, first overall pick, spent 14 minutes in the minor leagues. Like, ‘You just spoon-fed dude, like you didn’t have to grind. You didn’t play in the FCL as I did.’” The comment quickly became a talking point. When Skenes later heard what his teammate said, he gave a playful response of his own.“I don’t know what Bubba is talking about,” Skenes said. “I’m the one who needs to check my ego? Tell him to look in the mirror. Yeah I’m a grinder, contrary to what people think, I’m a grinder.” The moment showed the easygoing relationship between the two Pirates pitchers. While Skenes has already reached superstar status, he still enjoys the jokes inside the team.After a strong season with Pittsburgh, Skenes recently represented Team USA in the World Baseball Classic, adding another big milestone to his young career. Meanwhile, Chandler has been focusing on preparing for the upcoming season with the Pirates during Spring Training.In a recent Spring Training game against the Atlanta Braves, Chandler delivered an impressive outing. During the game, he threw 18 straight fastballs, all called by Pirates catcher Henry Davis. The idea was simple. Davis wanted to test Chandler’s control and see how accurate his fastball could be.“Plans aren’t a thing,” Chandler said. “Throw the first pitch, and every plan goes out the window. Whatever Henry called, I threw. It felt good coming out.” Chandler ended the game with strong numbers. He threw 66 pitches, with 47 strikes, and created 15 swings and misses. Out of those 66 pitches, 49 were four-seam fastballs.It was a strong step forward for Chandler after earlier struggles in Grapefruit League games. For the Pirates, moments like these show both competition and friendship inside the team. Players push each other to improve, but they also keep the mood light with jokes like the one Chandler made about Skenes.
Article Summary: Pittsburgh Pirates pitcher Paul Skenes responds after teammate Bubba Chandler jokes he was “spoon fed” in fast MLB rise. Chandler said the team enjoys reminding S Kenes about being the first overall pick in the MLB draft and how little time he spent in the minor leagues. The moment showed the light side of the Pirates' clubhouse.

Article Title: Nick Kyrgios love life: From Jannik Sinner's ex-girlfriend Anna Kalinskaya to Costeen Hatzi, inside the relationships of the tennis star
Article Text:
All anout Nick Kyrgios' relationships.Image via: X/ Getty

Dating Anna Kalinskaya and Australian tennis star Ajla Tomljanović

Nick Kyrgios has never done anything quietly. From the volleys he hits to the opinions he shares, everything about the Australian tennis star comes with volume. On the court, he has produced some of the most exciting shots the sport has witnessed in recent years. Off it, he has been just as interesting and just as unpredictable. A Wimbledon finalist in 2022 and a former top-20 player, Kyrgios has spent the better part of a decade as one of tennis's most talked-about personalities and that conversation has never been limited to what happens between the lines.His personal life has always drawn attention, partly because Kyrgios has never been particularly guarded about it. His candid social media presence and outspoken nature have meant that his relationships often play out at least partially in public view.Here is a look at the relationships that have shaped his story off the court.Nick Kyrgios' first well-known relationship was with fellow Australian tennis player Ajla Tomljanović. The two dated between 2015 and 2017. It was the kind of pairing that made natural sense. Two young Australians navigating the same world at the same time. They even partnered up for mixed doubles at the 2016 Australian Open, which gave fans a rare glimpse of them on the same side of the net.When they eventually went their separate ways, both continued building careers that would take them to very different places in the sport.After his relationship with Tomljanović ended, Kyrgios was linked to Russian tennis player Anna Kalinskaya, who later went out with Jannik Sinner. More publicly, he went on to date Australian influencer Chiara Passari from around 2020 to 2021. The relationship attracted a lot of attention and not always for the right reasons. Their breakup sparked a wave of headlines and legal proceedings that kept both names in the news long after the relationship itself had ended.The most significant relationship of Kyrgios' life so far began in one of the more unlikely ways you will come across. In late 2021, he came across Australian social media influencer Costeen Hatzi's Instagram page, where she had listed a mirror for sale. He sent her a message. That was how it started.What followed quickly became one of the most talked-about relationships in Australian tennis. Hatzi became a regular presence at his matches and was in the stands for some of the biggest moments of his career, including his remarkable run to the Wimbledon final in 2022. Their relationship also featured in the Netflix tennis series Break Point, which gave fans a genuinely personal look at Kyrgios away from the noise and the controversies. In 2025, Nick broke silence on their breakup and said he wished her well. He is now rumored to be dating Melbourne-based marketing manager Alexia Alessi.
Article Summary: Nick Kyrgios is one of the most talked-about tennis players in the world. His personal life has always drawn attention, partly because he has never been particularly guarded about it. He has dated Anna Kalinskaya, Ajla Tomljanović, Chiara Passari and Costeen Hatzi.

Article Title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding rumors spark royal level excitement as fans compare event to Princess Diana
Article Text:
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce (Image Via Getty)

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding plans spark global excitement as fans compare event to royal wedding

Swift has also spoken about the relationship in recent interviews. During a conversation with BBC Radio 2, she responded to suggestions that marriage might slow down her career. Swift said the idea was “shockingly offensive,” making it clear that she has no plans to step away from music.

She added that she and Kelce share a strong connection because both are highly competitive and passionate about their work. According to Swift, what Kelce calls “practice” in football feels similar to what she calls “rehearsal” before performing.

For now, the couple’s wedding plans continue to draw attention across the world. With Swift’s global fan base and Kelce’s popularity in the NFL, many people believe the ceremony could become one of the biggest celebrity events of the year.

The relationship between Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce has been one of the most talked about stories in sports and entertainment. Now the couple is moving closer to a major moment. Reports say Swift and Kelce are planning a wedding in mid June 2026, and the news has already created huge excitement among fans.According to reports, the ceremony is expected to take place at Taylor Swift’s Ocean House estate in Watch Hill, Rhode Island. The location has already seen increased hotel bookings as fans and media prepare for what many people believe could become one of the most watched celebrity weddings in recent years.Some commentators have even compared the global interest in the event to the famous 1981 wedding of Princess Diana and King Charles III.Interest in the wedding began growing after Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift announced their engagement in August 2025. Since then, the couple’s relationship has remained a major topic across sports and entertainment media.The timing of the wedding appears carefully planned. Reports suggest the couple chose a mid June ceremony so they could celebrate before the Kansas City Chiefs begin NFL training camp in late July.Kelce also recently made an important career decision. The star tight end signed a one year contract extension worth about $12 million to return for his 14th season with the Chiefs. According to people close to the situation, the couple worked together to find a date that would fit around Kelce’s football schedule.Media personality Justin Sylvester recently spoke about the public interest surrounding the wedding. While discussing the topic, he compared the level of attention to one of the most famous weddings in history.Sylvester said the excitement around Swift and Kelce reminds him of the 1981 wedding of Princess Diana and King Charles, noting how both partners bring huge global audiences.While fans focus on the upcoming wedding, both Swift and Kelce continue to talk openly about how they support each other’s careers.During an appearance on The Pat McAfee Show on March 10, Kelce explained that Swift’s dedication to her work inspired him to keep playing football instead of retiring.Kelce said seeing Swift’s commitment made him realize he still had the energy to compete. He explained that watching her continue creating music and performing made him feel like he still had “juice left” to play the sport he loves.
Article Summary: The wedding of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce is set to take place in June 2026. The wedding is expected to be one of the most watched events of the year. The couple have already signed a one-year contract extension with the Kansas City Chiefs.

Article Title: Iga Swiatek vs Elina Svitolina Indian Wells 2026 quarterfinal sparks outrage as Tennis Channel coverage leaves fans furious
Article Text:
Iga Swiatek vs Elina Svitolina (Image Via Getty)

Elina Svitolina shocks Iga Swiatek at Indian Wells as Tennis Channel coverage sparks backlash

Tennis fans expected a big moment when Iga Swiatek faced Elina Svitolina in the quarterfinals of the BNP Paribas Open at Indian Wells 2026. The match had everything people wanted from a top clash. But many viewers in the United States say they could not fully watch the action because of issues with the Tennis Channel broadcast.The problem quickly became a big talking point online. While the match delivered drama on court, fans complained that the television coverage did not properly show key moments. Many viewers went to social media to express their anger, saying a major quarterfinal between two top players deserved better coverage.On the court, Elina Svitolina delivered one of the most impressive wins of the tournament. The Ukrainian defeated Iga Swiatek 6–2, 4–6, 6–4 in a match that lasted two hours and nine minutes. With this victory, Svitolina reached the Indian Wells semifinals and improved her strong start to the 2026 season.The win was important for Svitolina because she had previously beaten Swiatek only once on the WTA Tour, which came at Wimbledon in 2023.After this result, her record in three set matches this season improved to 6–0, showing how strong she has been in long battles. Speaking after the match, Svitolina explained that her plan was to stay aggressive instead of waiting for mistakes.She said, “There is no champion who is waiting for mistakes. You really have to try to set yourself up in a good position to attack. I was feeling good and trying to open up the court and take my chances because Iga is such an aggressive player.” The turning point came late in the final set. With the score at 5–4, Svitolina broke Swiatek’s serve and then calmly served out the match to complete the upset.However, while the players delivered a thrilling contest, many fans said the Tennis Channel broadcast did not show the match properly. Viewers complained that studio discussion and other segments interrupted coverage of the quarterfinal.One fan wrote on social media, “Tennis Channel choosing to show Wertheim and Eubanks talking about men’s matches instead of showing the WTA quarterfinal is why sports media is so frustrating.”Another viewer questioned the network’s broadcast choice and wrote, “This feed should be exclusively Elina Svitolina vs Iga Swiatek. What is going on?” Some fans were even more direct. One post said, “Tennis Channel abandoning a top matchup at 2–2 in the final set so they can do desk talk, so unserious.”The criticism also reminded fans of earlier broadcast complaints. In the past, viewers have raised similar concerns during events like the Italian Open and Madrid Open, where matches were interrupted by ads or delayed feeds.For now, the result on the court is clear. Elina Svitolina moves into the Indian Wells semifinals, while the broadcast debate has created another discussion among tennis fans watching from home.
Article Summary: Elina Svitolina defeated Iga Swiatek 6–2, 4–6, 6–4 in the quarterfinals of the BNP Paribas Open at Indian Wells 2026. Many viewers in the United States say they could not fully watch the action because of issues with the Tennis Channel broadcast.

Article Title: Youtube Age Restriction: YouTuber Jacksepticeye slams YouTube moderation after Resident Evil Requiem gameplay gets age-restricted
Article Text:
Image Via Instagram

Jacksepticeye questions YouTube rules after only one Resident Evil Requiem video gets restricted

“inappropriate language.”

Jacksepticeye Youtube situation is crazy

“video homicide,”





YouTuber Seán McLoughlin has criticized YouTube after one of his gameplay videos was age-restricted while other videos from the same series were not. The creator was playing Resident Evil Requiem, the latest title in the popular horror franchise by Capcom. According to Jacksepticeye, only the fourth video in his five-part Let’s Play series was restricted, even though all the videos showed similar gameplay and content.Age restriction on YouTube means viewers must sign in to confirm their age before watching the video. This can reduce the number of views because some people may not be able to watch it easily. It can also limit advertisements, which may affect how much money the video earns.Jacksepticeye said the video was still doing well with around 1.4 million views, but he explained that age restrictions can still seriously hurt a video’s reach.The issue started when YouTube first told Jacksepticeye that the restriction happened because ofThe platform said the video might contain moderate profanity in the title, thumbnail, or throughout the video. However, the YouTuber said this explanation did not make sense.According to him, the title and thumbnail of the video did not include any swear words. He also said that his language in the video was similar to the other parts of the series, which were not restricted. Because of this, he felt confused about the reason behind the decision.After contacting his YouTube representative, Jacksepticeye received another explanation. This time, the platform said the video was restricted because it showed violent or graphic visuals. The message mentioned that videos showing certain violent scenes, medical procedures, animal abuse, or some gaming violence could break YouTube’s Community Guidelines.The YouTuber said the situation showed a bigger problem with consistency. He explained that many other creators are also playing Resident Evil Requiem, which features intense action scenes like shooting zombies or using weapons against enemies. However, many of those videos were not restricted.Jacksepticeye used the moment to explain how harmful age restrictions can be for creators. He even described it asmeaning it can severely damage how a video performs on the platform.So far, YouTube has not publicly responded to his criticism. However, it is important to note that Jacksepticeye is not the first YouTuber to voice his concerns regarding the inconsistent rules that moderators use. Over the years, various YouTubers have expressed that it is hard for them to comprehend the rules.
Article Summary: YouTuber Seán McLoughlin has criticized YouTube after one of his gameplay videos was age-restricted while other videos from the same series were not. The creator was playing Resident Evil Requiem, the latest title in the popular horror franchise by Capcom. According to Jacksepticeye, only the fourth video in his five-part Let's Play series was restricted.

Article Title: Serena Williams shares powerful body positivity message after transformation as 2026 tennis comeback rumors swirl
Article Text:
Serena Williams shares powerful body positivity message after transformation as 2026 tennis comeback rumors swirl (Image via Getty)

Serena Williams opens up about body image struggles and how she learned to ignore criticism



Serena Williams is once again getting people talking, but this time it is not about a tennis match. The 23-time Grand Slam champion recently shared a heartfelt message on Instagram about accepting her body and feeling comfortable in her own skin. The post came after fans noticed her physical transformation following a weight-loss journey that included changes in diet, exercise, and the use of GLP-1 medication.In the photos, Serena Williams stood outdoors near a pool, surrounded by trees. She wore an olive-green one-piece swimsuit, a soft robe, and tan slippers. Along with the photos, she shared a simple but powerful message about self-acceptance. Serena Williams wrote on Instagram, “I am not perfect….but I’m perfectly me.Take out time today to celebrate being perfectly you! 💋.”Serena Williams has spent decades in the public eye, winning 73 WTA singles titles and becoming one of the most successful players in tennis history. But despite all the victories, she has spoken honestly about a personal challenge that followed her throughout her career: body image.Early in her tennis journey, Serena Williams often looked different from many other players on the tour.Her powerful and muscular build stood out at a time when many female athletes were expected to look thinner. The difference sometimes brought criticism and unwanted comments.Speaking to Net-a-Porter in December, Serena Williams explained how those early years affected her mentally. She said, “It was hard because when I was playing in the beginning – the first 15 years – my body was different. I had big boobs; I had a big butt. Every athlete was like super flat, super thin and beautiful, but in a different way. And I didn’t understand as an athlete how to deal with that. It does affect you mentally. Absolutely.”Over time, Serena Williams says she learned to focus on her own mindset rather than outside opinions. The tennis legend explained that trying to change what others think is not worth the emotional stress.She told Net-a-Porter, “I don’t hear the noise. Everyone is entitled to their opinion. How am I going to sit here and change someone’s thought? If you don’t like me, you don’t have to. I’m not going to let anyone bring me down. I put enough stress on myself. The last thing I’m going to allow is someone else to do that.”Meanwhile, Serena Williams has also sparked fresh discussion in the tennis world for another reason. According to the International Tennis Integrity Agency testing pool list, her name appeared again in October. Under tennis rules, any player planning a comeback must spend six months in the testing pool before returning to competition.Because of that process, Serena Williams became eligible to return to professional tennis after February 22, 2026, though she has not officially announced any comeback plans.For now, her recent message on Instagram shows that Serena Williams is focusing on confidence, self-respect, and celebrating individuality, lessons that go far beyond tennis.
Article Summary: Serena Williams shared a powerful message about self-acceptance on Instagram. The 23-time Grand Slam champion recently underwent a weight-loss journey. Serena Williams has also sparked fresh discussion in the tennis world for another reason. According to the International Tennis Integrity Agency testing pool list, she is eligible to return to professional tennis after February 22, 2026.

Article Title: NHL trade update: Colorado Avalanche sign defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to entry-level contract
Article Text:
The Colorado Avalanche have signed defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to a two-year entry-level contract. (Image via Twitter/X)

Colorado Avalanche sign defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to an entry-level contract

The Colorado Avalanche have signed defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to a two-year contract after the Swedish blueliner completed his collegiate career at Bowling Green State University. This was announced by the club on Friday. As per reports, the deal will kick in for the start of the 2026-27 season, and Stjernberg will play on an AHL contract for the Avalanche’s minor-league affiliate, Colorado Eagles, for the remainder of this season. The exact financial details of the deal have not yet been released.According to reports, Stjernberg has spent the last three seasons with Bowling Green, where he also served as an alternate captain in the 2025-26 season.In 25 games this year, the Swedish defenseman recorded six goals and four assists for 10 points. Over his three-year collegiate career, he totaled 14 goals and 23 assists for 37 points in 86 games.The Colorado Avalanche have recently signed the Swedish defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to a two-year entry-level contract. The deal begins in the 2026-27 season, while Stjernberg will join the AHL’s Colorado Eagles for the rest of the current campaign on a separate contract.The club announced the same on Friday, and also noted that Stjernberg’s NHL deal will kick in next season, giving the organization a chance to get an early look at him in the AHL before he officially becomes part of their NHL roster picture. However, after the announcement of the signing of the defenseman, the financial terms were not disclosed, and are still in line with team policy.Stjernberg is considered a coming off player, who has played for a three-year career at Bowling Green State University. As for his performance, in 25 games this season serving as an alternate captain, the In addition to this, Swedish national also had six goals and four assists for 10 points. Last season, he put up four goals and 12 assists for 16 points in 30 games.Over 86 NCAA games, the right-shot defender totaled 14 goals and 23 assists for 37 points. Before heading to college, he played one season in the USHL with the Des Moines Buccaneers, putting up 20 points (eight goals, 12 assists) in 62 games.In the 2023-24 season, his first for Bowling Green, he registered four goals and seven assists for 11 points in 31 games. In his collegiate career, Stjernberg compiled 14 goals and 23 assists for 37 points in 86 games played. His strong hold on the game and impressive career stats made him join the league with the Colorado Avalanche.
Article Summary: Colorado Avalanche sign defenseman Gustav Stjernberg to an entry-level contract. The Swedish blueliner completed his collegiate career at Bowling Green State University. The deal will kick in for the start of the 2026-27 season. The financial details of the deal have not yet been released. In 25 games this year, the Swedish defenseman recorded six goals and four assists for 10 points.

Article Title: Donald Trump proposes unexpected Jake Paul vs Khabib Nurmagomedov fight despite retired UFC legend standing firm on retirement
Article Text:
Donald Trump proposes unexpected Jake Paul vs Khabib Nurmagomedov fight despite retired UFC legend standing firm on retirement (Image via Getty)

Donald Trump tells Jake Paul he should fight retired UFC champion Khabib Nurmagomedov



Donald Trump has thrown out a surprising idea for a future fight. He told Jake Paul that he should try to bring UFC legend Khabib Nurmagomedov out of retirement. Khabib Nurmagomedov walked away from MMA in 2020 as the UFC lightweight champion. He finished his career with a perfect 29 wins and no losses. Since then, he has stayed firm on his decision to retire. Even huge offers have not changed his mind. One of those offers was reportedly worth $100 million to fight boxing icon Floyd Mayweather.Still, Donald Trump believes a fight between Jake Paul and Khabib Nurmagomedov would be interesting to watch. Jake Paul has already built a reputation by fighting former MMA stars in boxing matches.Over the past few years he has beaten fighters like Anderson Silva, Nate Diaz, and Ben Askren. He also knocked out Tyron Woodley in their rematch. Now, Donald Trump thinks the next name on that list should be Khabib Nurmagomedov.The unexpected suggestion came during a conversation between Donald Trump and Jake Paul at a rally event supporting Trump. According to Jake Paul, they later recorded a podcast-style talk which he described as “controversial.”During the conversation, they started talking about combat sports. Donald Trump has often spoken about his interest in boxing and MMA.Jake Paul was asked about his next opponent. He explained that he wants to stay close to the cruiserweight division in boxing, or possibly try MMA in the future. While discussing possible fights, he mentioned several big names. These included Oleksandr Usyk, Francis Ngannou, and Ryan Garcia.Then Jake Paul asked Donald Trump who he would like to see him fight.Trump responded directly:“I would probably say Khabib. When people say he was the best, he really was great.”Jake Paul did not reject the idea. Instead, he said he would be open to it, though another possible opponent being discussed is Tommy Fury.Paul also gave an update about his health. He said his jaw is still recovering from his last fight with Anthony Joshua, a bout he said he even watched again on Netflix while traveling with Donald Trump on Air Force One.According to Jake Paul, doctors have told him that full-contact sparring may not be possible until later in the year.However, he sounded confident about the recovery. Jake Paul said, “My jaw is healing and it might actually come back stronger.”For now, the idea of Jake Paul fighting Khabib Nurmagomedov remains only a suggestion. Khabib has repeated many times that he is fully retired. Still, Donald Trump’s unexpected call for the fight has already sparked plenty of discussion among fight fans.
Article Summary: Donald Trump has suggested a fight between Jake Paul and Khabib Nurmagomedov. The idea came during a conversation between Donald Trump and Jake Paul at a rally event supporting Trump. Jake Paul has built a reputation by fighting former MMA stars in boxing matches.

Article Title: Rawalpindi Traffic Police trolls Pakistan batter Salman Ali Agha after controversial run-out vs Bangladesh
Article Text:
Image credit: Bangladesh Cricket Board

Ishan Kishan responds to Kirti Azad's criticism | T20 World Cup

NEW DELHI: A dramatic moment in the second ODI between Pakistan national cricket team and Bangladesh national cricket team sparked huge debate online after Salman Ali Agha was involved in a controversial run-out.The incident quickly went viral, and even the Rawalpindi Traffic Police joined the conversation with a safety message inspired by the moment.During Pakistan’s innings, Agha was batting on 64 when the dramatic dismissal happened. Mohammad Rizwan played a shot towards bowler Mehidy Hasan Miraz.The ball deflected off Agha, and both batters tried to take a run. However, Miraz reacted quickly, picked up the ball and hit the stumps, catching Agha short of his crease.The decision led to intense reactions on social media, with many fans debating whether the dismissal was fair or against the spirit of the game.The situation became even more dramatic when Agha showed visible frustration while leaving the field. He threw his helmet and gloves and angrily gestured towards the Bangladeshi bowler, creating a moment that quickly spread across the internet.Soon after, Rawalpindi Traffic Police used the viral incident to deliver a message about safety and personal responsibility.Sharing an image from the match, they posted a reminder that awareness and quick thinking are important both in sports and everyday life.“Whether on the road or on the playing field, safety begins with personal responsibility. Trust yourself, stay alert, and make wise decisions to keep yourself and others safe,” it wrote on X.The police also added that depending completely on others can sometimes be risky. They stressed that making careful decisions, such as following traffic rules and staying aware of surroundings, helps protect everyone.
Article Summary: Salman Ali Agha was involved in a controversial run-out during Pakistan's second ODI match against Bangladesh. The decision led to intense reactions on social media, with many fans debating whether the dismissal was fair or against the spirit of the game. Rawalpindi Traffic Police used the viral incident to deliver a message about safety.

Article Title: After Kirti Azad’s criticism, Suryakumar Yadav, Gautam Gambhir visit temple with T20 World Cup again - Watch
Article Text:
Gautam Gambhir, SuryaKumar Yadav offer prayers at Shree Siddhivinayak Temple

Sanju Samson opens up on T20 World Cup journey, and title win

Kirti Azad questions temple visit

Harbhajan Singh

criticises Azad’s remarks

Gambhir also defends team’s celebration

Players brush aside controversy

Members of the India national cricket team continued their celebrations of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup triumph by visiting the Shree Siddhivinayak Temple in Mumbai on Saturday, even as their previous temple visit had triggered a debate following remarks by former cricketer-turned-politician Kirti Azad.Head coach Gautam Gambhir and captain Suryakumar Yadav along side ICC Chairman Jay shah had earlier visited the temple with the trophy in Ahmedabad after the victory and, offering prayers despite the controversy surrounding the earlier visit.The debate began after Kirti Azad criticised the team’s decision to take the trophy to a temple, arguing that the World Cup victory belonged to all Indians regardless of religion.“140 crore people were excited when Team India, which includes people from all religions, won.A sportsperson or sports does not belong to any religion or caste but only to the sport,” Azad said.“Why not a mosque? Why not a church? Why not a gurudwara? The trophy belongs to 1.4 billion Indians of every faith, not one religion’s victory lap,” he added.Former India spinner Harbhajan Singh strongly criticised Azad’s comments, accusing him of unnecessarily politicising the issue.“It’s absurd that someone is politicising the players taking the trophy to a temple.Kirti himself is a former cricketer, so I didn’t expect him to say such a thing. Perhaps he’s prioritising politics more and has forgotten he’s a sportsman,” Harbhajan said.“I was expecting more because when such a big task is undertaken, the reward should be even greater. But congratulations to the team for showing everyone how powerful India is,” he added.Gautam Gambhir also defended the team’s actions, saying the controversy was unnecessary and risked overshadowing the players’ achievements.“It is not even worth answering this question. It’s a huge moment for the entire country and we should celebrate the World Cup winners,” Gambhir said in a conversation with ANI.“If you want to dilute the achievements of those 15 players and their efforts, then tomorrow anyone can give any statement and we start taking it seriously. That isn’t fair to the boys.”“The boys have gone through so much pressure. If you give such statements today, you are degrading your own players and your own team.”Meanwhile, India batter Ishan Kishan dismissed questions about the controversy, urging the media to focus on the team’s historic achievement instead.“Winning the World Cup is such a great thing. You people should ask good questions. What can I say about what Kirti Azad said? Please ask good questions so that it’s enjoyable,” Kishan said.
Article Summary: Members of the India national cricket team continued their celebrations of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup triumph by visiting the Shree Siddhivinayak Temple in Mumbai. Their previous temple visit had triggered a debate following remarks by former cricketer-turned-politician Kirti Azad. Head coach Gautam Gambhir and captain Suryakumar Yadav along side ICC Chairman Jay shah had earlier visited the temple with the trophy.

Article Title: Watch: MS Dhoni, Ruturaj Gaikwad blast bowlers; video goes viral ahead of IPL 2026
Article Text:
Ruturaj Gaikwad and Ruturaj Gaikwad (Screengrabs)

IPL 2026 should be audition for the next India T20I captain

NEW DELHI: Chennai Super Kings have begun early preparations for the upcoming Indian Premier League 2026 season, focusing on fitness and helping new players settle into the squad. The early training camp is aimed at building momentum as the team looks to bounce back after missing the playoffs in the last two seasons.A video shared by CSK on social media has already created excitement among fans. The clip shows veteran star MS Dhoni and captain Ruturaj Gaikwad batting together in the nets, smashing bowlers during practice.The footage quickly went viral online, giving supporters a glimpse of the duo’s strong form ahead of the new season.Watch:CSK, one of the most successful teams in IPL history with five titles, are determined to return to winning ways.After a couple of disappointing seasons, the franchise is now targeting a record sixth IPL trophy.The squad has a mix of experienced players and young talent, and the addition of Sanju Samson has further strengthened their lineup.The early practice sessions by Dhoni and Gaikwad highlight the team’s determination and serious approach. Their commitment to training well before the season begins sends a strong message to rivals that CSK are eager to compete at the highest level again.CSK will start their IPL 2026 campaign against Rajasthan Royals on March 30 in Guwahati. The first phase of the tournament, announced by the Board of Control for Cricket in India, will run from March 28 to April 12, with 20 matches scheduled across 10 cities.During this phase, CSK will also play home matches in Chennai against Punjab Kings and Delhi Capitals, as they aim to make a strong start to their IPL 2026 campaign.

Article Title: 'That's all you need': Ricky Ponting reveals 'big decision' behind India's T20 World Cup title
Article Text:
Ricky Ponting speaks about Team India's T20 World Cup-winning campaign

IPL 2026 should be audition for the next India T20I captain

NEW DELHI: Former Australia captain Ricky Ponting has praised India’s decision to back Sanju Samson as an opener during their victorious campaign in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup. According to Ponting, the trust shown in Samson by captain Suryakumar Yadav and the coaching staff played a major role in India’s success.Samson’s tournament did not start smoothly. Before the World Cup, he had scored only 46 runs in five T20Is against New Zealand national cricket team. He initially got a chance in the league match against Namibia national cricket team in New Delhi after Abhishek Sharma fell ill. Later, he was brought back for a key Super Eight game against Zimbabwe national cricket team and turned his tournament around with a series of brilliant performances.Samson played some crucial knocks as India advanced in the competition. He scored an unbeaten 97 against West Indies national cricket team in Kolkata, followed by 89 in the semifinal against England national cricket team in Mumbai and another 89 in the final against New Zealand national cricket team in Ahmedabad. His total of 321 runs in five innings became the most by an Indian batter in a single T20 World Cup edition and earned him the Player of the Tournament award.“For India to stick with him at the top there, that was a big call to make. A big decision, but one that worked out really well in the end. If you get the backing of the coaching staff and the captain, then that's all you need,” Ponting said on The ICC Review show.“Just a pat on the back or an arm around the shoulder to say, ‘we're sticking with you, we believe in you.’ When you've got the quality that Sanju's got, and the confidence of the captain and coach behind you, that's when great things can happen.”Ponting also praised Suryakumar’s leadership and how he managed his players during the tournament. “It would have been really interesting to see how Surya has interacted with Abhishek (Sharam) and Sanju (Samson) over the last few weeks. That’s where the real stories will come out on true leadership.”Despite not scoring heavily himself apart from an unbeaten 84 against the United States national cricket team, Suryakumar guided India to the title.Ponting concluded, “It's about a lot more than what they do off the field, the things that people don't see and how they interact with their players. He didn’t have a great time himself as a player, but still, he’s standing at the end holding up a World Cup trophy.”
Article Summary: Ricky Ponting has praised India's decision to back Sanju Samson as an opener during their victorious campaign in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup. The trust shown in Samson by captain Suryakumar Yadav and the coaching staff played a major role in India’s success.
